extraction.one_line_summary: EWAN Caleb won the Champs-Élysées sprint with a long-range acceleration from far back, beating GROENEWEGEN Dylan on the line.
tactical_patterns: EWAN launched sprint from exceptionally far back on Champs-Élysées, contradicting conventional wisdom about late jumps on uphill finish.
MATTHEWS Michael's mechanical 12km out destroyed sprint preparation despite regaining peloton, showing fragility of sprint leadouts under time pressure.
VIVIANI Elia lost positioning despite MORKOV and Quick-Step controlling front, revealing Deceuninck train vulnerable to narrow Champs-Élysées geometry.
BOASSON HAGEN Edvald's premature long-range move drew out peloton before final corner, sacrificing dimension data's positioning for nothing.

rider_signals.EWAN Caleb: positive: third stage win demonstrates Lotto-Soudal's superior sprint timing execution versus more powerful Quick-Step train
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: neutral: positioned identically far back as Ewan but lost by centimeters, suggesting near-perfect execution insufficient against peak Ewan
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: out of position despite strongest leadout train indicates positional awareness deficiency on technical finishes
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: mechanical at 12km eliminated contention even after rejoining, confirming poor 2019 Tour luck continues
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: negative: premature acceleration above Arc de Triomphe wasted dimension data's front position and own energy reserves
